step_by_step_thinking:
The denominator for calculating the perinatal mortality rate should include all pregnancies that end with either a stillbirth or a neonate who dies within one month after birth. This encompasses both live births and stillbirths, as it measures overall pregnancy outcomes including those that do not result in survival beyond this period.

My Answer Choice is C: Live bihs + Still bihs To calculate the perinatal mortality rate accurately, we must consider all adverse pregnancy outcomes, which includes stillbirths (deaths before delivery) and deaths occurring up to 28 days post-delivery among live-born infants.
raw_response:
The denominator for calculating the perinatal mortality rate should include all pregnancies that end with either a stillbirth or a neonate who dies within one month after birth. This encompasses both live births and stillbirths, as it measures overall pregnancy outcomes including those that do not result in survival beyond this period.

My Answer Choice is C: Live bihs + Still bihs To calculate the perinatal mortality rate accurately, we must consider all adverse pregnancy outcomes, which includes stillbirths (deaths before delivery) and deaths occurring up to 28 days post-delivery among live-born infants. Therefore, my answer is C: Live births + Still births.
